sessions.outcome: The logs aren't missing anything — **June 17 is genuinely day one**. There are only 3 days of transcripts because that's when Claude Code was first set up on this machine. The historical view is showing everything that exists.

summary:
Agents - Designed and tested an agent-to-backlog handoff model, including a live progressive-disclosure lesson on agent fit and tool boundaries.

Automation - Built a two-stage session reporting pipeline separating a zero-cost scheduled extraction layer from optional on-demand LLM summarization.

Loops - Hardened a scheduled job (logging, error recovery, cron UI refinement) into a more reliable recurring automation loop.

Focused on building the operational scaffolding (agent boundaries, automated reporting, scheduled job reliability) needed to run AI-assisted workflows unattended and at scale.

Count of session: 6
Count of automations ran: 2
